title: Barn Lamp Light Through Broken Glass Material Lab
description: The user stands in a dark scene where an anisotropic barn lamp illuminates a broken glass window panel. The user must configure the lamp's light properties and then adjust the broken glass material parameters to achieve a specific target refraction and caustic appearance, validating each parameter against threshold ranges across multiple interaction steps.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that loads AnisotropyBarnLamp.glb and GlassBrokenWindow.glb using GLTFLoader. Position the barn lamp on the left at (-2, 0, 0) and the broken glass window upright on the right at (1.5, 1, 0), facing the lamp. Set the camera at (0, 2, 5) looking at the origin with OrbitControls enabled. Start the scene mostly dark with a dim ambient light (intensity 0.05). Add a PointLight parented near the lamp's bulb area, initially off (intensity 0). Create a dat.gui panel with these sequenced controls the user must adjust: (1) Lamp Power slider 0-10, default 0 — sets the PointLight intensity. (2) Lamp Color picker, default white — sets PointLight color. (3) Light Distance slider 1-20, default 5 — sets PointLight distance. (4) Glass Opacity slider 0-1, default 1 — iterates all meshes in the broken window model and sets material.opacity and material.transparent accordingly. (5) Glass Roughness slider 0-1, default 0.5 — sets material.roughness on all glass meshes. (6) Glass IOR slider 1.0-2.5, default 1.5 — sets material.ior if the material supports it (MeshPhysicalMaterial). (7) Glass Transmission slider 0-1, default 0 — sets material.transmission on glass meshes. (8) A 'Validate Scene' button. When clicked, check all parameters against target ranges: Lamp Power 4-7, Light Distance 8-15, Glass Opacity 0.2-0.5, Glass Roughness 0.05-0.2, Glass IOR 1.4-1.6, Glass Transmission 0.7-1.0. Display a floating HTML overlay showing pass/fail for each parameter with green/red indicators. If all pass, show a golden 'PERFECT CONFIGURATION' banner with a subtle CSS glow animation. Ensure all glass materials are converted to MeshPhysicalMaterial if they are not already, preserving original maps. Add a subtle environment map using PMREMGenerator with a simple procedural RoomEnvironment (from three/addons) so reflections and transmission render correctly. The floor should be a simple dark plane with MeshStandardMaterial receiving shadows. Enable renderer shadow mapping with the point light casting shadows.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  lampPowerIntensity: number,         // current intensity of the barn lamp's PointLight (0-10)
  lampColorHex: string,         // hex color string of the lamp's PointLight (e.g. '#ffffff')
  lightDistanceValue: number,         // distance parameter of the PointLight (1-20)
  glassOpacityValue: number,         // opacity of all broken glass meshes (0-1)
  glassRoughnessValue: number,         // roughness of all broken glass materials (0-1)
  glassIorValue: number,         // index of refraction for glass materials (1.0-2.5)
  glassTransmissionValue: number,         // transmission value for glass materials (0-1)
  lampModelLoaded: boolean,         // whether AnisotropyBarnLamp.glb has loaded successfully
  glassModelLoaded: boolean,         // whether GlassBrokenWindow.glb has loaded successfully
  totalGlassMeshCount: number,         // count of meshes in the broken glass window model
  pointLightActive: boolean,         // whether the PointLight intensity is greater than zero
}
